source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Lifting Incident at Green Canyon Block 52 A Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 52

actions_taken: Operation stopped when stinger latched to boom lace
Crane taken out of service by BSEE on June 6, 2024
Independent crane inspector derated lifting capacity to 50% on June 10, 2024
Crane boom tip replaced and load tested on June 16, 2024
event.summary: On May 31, 2024 at 01:00 hrs, during offloading and backloading operations at GC 52 A Platform, a stinger attached to the crane's main block latched into a lace on the crane boom. Crane Operator 2 (CO-2) observed this and stopped operations, notifying the Person In Charge (PIC). The PIC instructed Crane Operator 1 (CO-1) to lower only the boom without lowering the main block, contrary to CO-2's recommendation to lower both simultaneously. As the boom was lowered, the stinger pulled tight and snapped a weld on the boom lace. The damaged crane continued in operation until June 6, 2024, when BSEE inspectors removed it from service. The boom tip was replaced at a cost of $70,000.
recommendations: Remove stinger when not in use
Enhance pre-use crane inspection procedures to identify loose gear
Improve Job Safety Analysis to cover possibility of stinger hanging up on boom
Implement and enforce Stop Work Authority (SWA) protocols
Ensure supervisory personnel listen to and act on safety concerns raised by crew

hazards.name: Loose gear on crane (stinger left on main block)

threats.name: Stinger latching to boom lace during lifting operations
Improper boom lowering procedure creating tension on stinger
Continued operation of damaged crane with reduced capacity

consequences.name: Structural damage to crane boom (broken weld on lace)
Potential for load drop or equipment failure during subsequent lifts

controls.name: Pre-use crane inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identify loose gear and equipment defects before operations
controls.lod_basis: Routine inspection procedure to catch hazards before they manifest

supporting_text: Although a pre-use crane inspection was performed, the stinger attached to the main block was not identified as loose gear
Step 6 of the pre-use crane inspection is to visually check for loose gear such as slings, hooks, and shackles

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and safe work procedures for lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Planning tool to anticipate and mitigate operational hazards

supporting_text: The JSA did not cover the possibility of the stinger hanging up on the boom
Management systems – The JSA was inadequate. It did not specify the stinger possibly hanging up on boom

controls.name: Supervision and operational decision-making
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe procedures are followed during lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Real-time oversight to prevent unsafe actions during operations

supporting_text: The PIC signaled for CO-1 to lower the boom without slacking off the main block. CO-2 advised the PIC to lower the boom and main block at the same time. The PIC ignored the warning continued to signal CO-1 to only lower the boom
Supervision – The PIC failed to address the concerns of CO-2 when lowering the boom without lowering the main block

controls.name: Stop Work Authority (SWA)
controls.barrier_role: Halt unsafe operations when hazards are identified
controls.lod_basis: Emergency control to prevent continuation of unsafe work

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority (SWA) was not used when the PIC failed to listen to CO-2 recommendation of lowering the boom and main block at the same time
Communication – Stop Work Authority (SWA) was not used when the boom was lowered without lowering the big block at the same time

controls.name: Equipment removal from service when damaged
controls.barrier_role: Prevent use of damaged equipment that poses safety risk
controls.lod_basis: Immediate action to prevent consequences from damaged equipment

supporting_text: The following day the damaged lace was found on a crane pre-use inspection. CO-2 informed the PIC about the broken lace and he was instructed to keep using the crane

The PIC told the CO-2 to keep using the crane. Next, CO-2 asked about the diminished capacity of the crane and the PIC again told him to keep using the crane as is. The damaged crane stayed in operation from May 31, 2024, until June 6, 2024

controls.name: Regulatory inspection and enforcement
controls.barrier_role: Identify and enforce removal of unsafe equipment from service
controls.lod_basis: External oversight to catch failures in internal controls

supporting_text: The crane was taken out of service by the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) Houma District inspectors on June 6, 2024
The crane was uncovered on an inspection conducted by BSEE Houma District inspectors on June 6, 2024

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
